 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.1","title":"Create acfs.manifest.yaml with all modules","description":"# Task: Create Complete Module Manifest

## Description
Create the canonical manifest file with all 40+ tool definitions.

## File: acfs.manifest.yaml

## Structure

```yaml
version: 1
name: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
id: acfs

defaults:
  user: ubuntu
  workspace_root: /data/projects
  mode: vibe
  
categories:
  - id: base
    name: System Fundamentals
    priority: 1
  - id: shell
    name: Shell Environment
    priority: 2
  # ... etc

modules:
  # Base system
  - id: base.curl
    description: HTTP client
    category: base
    install: [sudo apt-get install -y curl]
    verify: [curl --version]
    
  # ... 40+ modules
```

## Module Categories

1. **base** - System packages (curl, git, jq, etc.)
2. **users** - User/permissions setup
3. **shell** - zsh, oh-my-zsh, plugins, theme
4. **cli** - Modern CLI tools (lsd, bat, fzf, etc.)
5. **lang** - Language runtimes (bun, uv, rust, go)
6. **tools** - Dev tools (atuin, tmux, ast-grep)
7. **db** - Databases (postgres18)
8. **cloud** - Cloud CLIs (vault, wrangler, supabase, vercel)
9. **agents** - Coding agents (claude, codex, gemini)
10. **stack** - Dicklesworthstone tools (8 tools)

## Each Module Contains
- id: Stable identifier
- description: Short description
- category: Category reference
- install: List of install commands
- verify: List of verify commands
- aliases: Optional shell aliases
- docs_url: Documentation link
- dependencies: Other module IDs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 40+ modules defined
- [ ] Valid YAML syntax
- [ ] All verify commands documented
- [ ] Dependencies specified
- [ ] Categories organized logically","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:11.851933-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:11:00.620729-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:11:00.620729-05:00","close_reason":"acfs.manifest.yaml exists with 287 lines covering all tools","labels":["manifest","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:11.853698-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.2","title":"Build manifest parser library (packages/manifest)","description":"# Task: Build Manifest Parser Library

## Description
Create a TypeScript library to parse and work with acfs.manifest.yaml.

## Location
`packages/manifest/`

## Exports

```typescript
// Parse manifest
export function parseManifest(yamlPath: string): Manifest;

// Types
export interface Manifest {
  version: number;
  name: string;
  id: string;
  defaults: ManifestDefaults;
  categories: Category[];
  modules: Module[];
}

export interface Module {
  id: string;
  description: string;
  category: string;
  install: string[];
  verify: string[];
  aliases?: string[];
  docs_url?: string;
  dependencies?: string[];
}

// Utilities
export function getModulesByCategory(manifest: Manifest, category: string): Module[];
export function getModuleDependencies(manifest: Manifest, moduleId: string): Module[];
export function validateManifest(manifest: Manifest): ValidationResult;
```

## Features
- YAML parsing with proper error messages
- Schema validation
- Dependency cycle detection
- Type-safe access

## Dependencies
- yaml (parsing)
- zod (validation)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parses valid manifest
- [ ] Rejects invalid manifest with clear errors
- [ ] Type definitions exported
- [ ] Utilities work correctly
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:23.533183-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:52:26.631736-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:52:26.631736-05:00","close_reason":"Completed manifest parser library with types, schema, parser, utils, and exports. All tests pass.","labels":["manifest","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:23.53483-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.3","title":"Create manifest-to-installer generator","description":"# Task: Manifest-to-Installer Generator

## Description
Generate installer module scripts from acfs.manifest.yaml.

## Purpose
The manifest is the single source of truth. The installer shouldn't
duplicate this information - it should be generated.

## What It Generates

### For each module category, generate:
```bash
# scripts/generated/install_<category>.sh
# Auto-generated from acfs.manifest.yaml - DO NOT EDIT

install_base() {
    log_step "Installing base.curl"
    sudo apt-get install -y curl
    verify "curl --version"
    
    log_step "Installing base.git"
    # ... etc
}
```

### For doctor checks:
```bash
# scripts/generated/doctor_checks.sh
CHECKS=(
    "identity.user_is_ubuntu|Logged in as ubuntu|[[ \$(whoami) == 'ubuntu' ]]"
    # ... etc
)
```

## Implementation
- packages/manifest/generate.ts
- Reads acfs.manifest.yaml
- Outputs to scripts/generated/

## CI Integration
- Run generator on manifest changes
- Fail if generated files differ (ensure they're committed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Generator script works
- [ ] Installer modules generated
- [ ] Doctor checks generated
- [ ] CI validates generated files","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:38.206152-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:55:41.728724-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:55:41.728724-05:00","close_reason":"Generator working: 11 category installers + doctor_checks.sh + install_all.sh generated from manifest","labels":["manifest","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.3","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:38.208032-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-g29","title":"Fix shellcheck + typecheck for generator outputs","description":"ShellCheck and TS type-check were failing due to generator using import.meta.path, and generated scripts triggering SC1091/SC2034. Fix generator, logging shim, and generated scripts so CI passes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T13:05:22.156507-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:05:58.659374-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:05:58.659374-05:00","close_reason":"Fix generator type errors + make generated scripts ShellCheck-clean (SC1091/SC2034)","labels":["ci","manifest"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hii","title":"Create VERSION file and version management","description":"# Task: Create VERSION File and Version Management

## Description
Set up version management for ACFS releases.

## VERSION File
Root of repo: `VERSION`
```
0.1.0
```

## Version Format
Semantic versioning: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH
- MAJOR: Breaking changes to installer
- MINOR: New features
- PATCH: Bug fixes

## Version Usage

### In installer
```bash
ACFS_VERSION=$(curl -fsSL .../VERSION)
```

### In acfs doctor
```
ACFS Doctor v0.1.0
```

### In website
Show version in footer

### In manifest
```yaml
version: 1
acfs_version: 0.1.0
```

## Release Process
1. Update VERSION file
2. Update CHANGELOG.md
3. Tag release: `git tag v0.1.0`
4. Push: `git push --tags`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VERSION file created
- [ ] All components read version correctly
- [ ] Version shown in doctor output
- [ ] Version shown on website","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:50.115466-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:10:59.74241-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:10:59.74241-05:00","close_reason":"VERSION file exists with 0.1.0","labels":["infrastructure","task"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kwk","title":"Vendor NTM command_palette.md for onboarding","description":"# Task: Vendor NTM Command Palette

## Description
Copy and maintain a local version of NTM's command_palette.md for use in onboarding.

## Background
The onboard TUI needs to show NTM's prompt palette to users. Rather than fetch it live (requires internet), we vendor a copy.

## Implementation

### Vendor Location
`acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/command_palette.md`

### Sync Script
`scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh`

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Sync NTM command palette from upstream

SOURCE="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/command_palette.md"
DEST="acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/command_palette.md"

curl -fsSL "$SOURCE" > "$DEST"
echo "Synced command_palette.md"
```

### Install Location
Deployed to: `~/.acfs/docs/ntm/command_palette.md`

### Onboard Integration
In Mission 6, show the palette using:
- bat (pretty) if available
- less as fallback

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sync script works
- [ ] Palette included in installer
- [ ] Displayed in onboard Mission 6
- [ ] Readable over SSH","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:28:15.931606-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:22:39.515282-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:22:39.515282-05:00","close_reason":"Vendored NTM command_palette.md to acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/. Added sync script scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh with --check mode. Commit 2a09af8.","labels":["onboard","task"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mwh","title":"Create VPS provider guides (OVH, Contabo, Hetzner)","description":"# Task: Create VPS Provider Guides

## Description
Create detailed provider-specific guides with screenshots.

## Location
- scripts/providers/ovh.md
- scripts/providers/contabo.md
- scripts/providers/hetzner.md

## Each Guide Contains

### Provider Overview
- Price range for recommended specs
- Pros/cons
- Direct signup link

### Step-by-Step with Screenshots
1. Navigate to VPS section
2. Select Ubuntu 25.x image
3. Choose specs (~$50/mo tier)
4. Add SSH key (screenshot of where)
5. Create server
6. Find IP address (screenshot)

### Provider Quirks
- Default username (root vs ubuntu)
- Firewall setup needed?
- Any DNS/network setup needed?

## Screenshot Placeholders
Use consistent naming:
- ovh-step1-select-os.png
- ovh-step2-add-ssh-key.png
- etc.

## Integration
Website wizard step 5 loads these guides dynamically.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Guide for OVH
- [ ] Guide for Contabo
- [ ] Guide for Hetzner
- [ ] Screenshot placeholders
- [ ] Consistent format","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:16.271045-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:25:21.350296-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:25:21.350296-05:00","close_reason":"Created comprehensive VPS setup guides for OVH, Contabo, and Hetzner. Each guide includes provider overview, pros/cons, step-by-step instructions, and provider-specific notes. Screenshot placeholders included. Commit 8c725ed.","labels":["documentation","task"]}
